headlines.title: Can Gilead's Efforts to Develop New Treatments Solidify HIV Franchise?
headlines.summary: GILD's HIV franchise gains momentum with strong Biktarvy and Descovy demand, Yeztugo growth and a promising pipeline targeting long-term expansion.

headlines.title: Best Monthly Dividend ETFs for Consistent Income
headlines.summary: If you're looking for more frequent payouts, you can still enjoy the benefits of low fees and broad diversification, thanks to these top monthly dividend ETFs.

headlines.title: Merck (MRK) Could Be 4% Undervalued As LIPFENDRA Approval Tests Its Premium Valuation
headlines.summary: Merck (MRK) is back in focus for investors after the US Food and Drug Administration approved LIPFENDRA, the first oral PCSK9 inhibitor. This adds a new cardiovascular drug to the company’s portfolio. See our latest analysis for Merck. Merck’s share price is $127.47, with a 30-day share price return of 10.38% and a year-to-date share price return of 19.75%. The 1-year total shareholder return of 56.86% reflects strong momentum alongside frequent product and trial updates.

headlines.title: Why Retirees Are Choosing This $100.8 Billion ETF Over Individual Dividend Stocks
headlines.summary: SCHD's 100-stock structure and rock-bottom fees make it a retiree favorite, but a closer look at its top four holdings reveals one company sitting on a patent cliff that could quietly reshape its income story after 2028.

headlines.title: Organon & Co. Shareholders Clear Sun Pharma Merger Deal
headlines.summary: Shareholders of Organon & Co. (NYSE:OGN) approved the company’s proposed merger agreement with Sun Pharmaceutical Holdings USA, Inc. at a virtual special meeting held Wednesday, according to preliminary voting results announced during the meeting.

description: Merck & Co., Inc. operates as a healthcare company worldwide. It offers human health pharmaceutical for various areas under the Keytruda, Keytruda Qlex, Welireg, Gardasil, ProQuad, M-M-R II, Varivax, Vaxneuvance, Capvaxive, RotaTeq, Pneumovax 23, Bridion, Prevymis, Dificid, Zerbaxa, Winrevair, Adempas/ Verquvo, Ohtuvayre, Lagevrio, Isentress/Isentress HD, Delstrigo, Pifeltro, Belsomra, Januvia, and Janumet brands. The company also provides veterinary pharmaceuticals, vaccines and health management solutions and services, such as livestock products under the Nuflor, Bovilis/Vista, Bovilis Cryptium, Banamine, Estrumate, Matrix, Resflor, Zuprevo, Revalor, Safe-Guard, M+Pac, Porcilis, Circumvent, Nobilis/Innovax, Paracox and Coccivac, Exzolt, Slice, Imvixa, Clynav, Aquavac/Norvax, Aquaflor, Flexolt brands; Allflex Livestock Intelligence solutions; and companion animal products under the Bravecto One-Month, Bravecto Injectable/Quantum, Bravecto TriUNO, Bravecto Plus, Sentinel Spectrum, Sentinel Flavor Tabs, Numelvi, Optimmune, Nobivac NXT, GilvetMab, Otomax, Mometamax, Mometamax Ultra, Posatex, Caninsulin/Vetsulin, Panacur, Safeguard, Regumate, Prestige, Scalibor/Exspot, Sure Petcare, and Home Again brands. It has development and commercialization agreement for three of Daiichi Sankyo's deruxtecan ADC candidates; AstraZeneca PLC to co-development and co-commercialize AstraZeneca's Lynparza products for multiple cancer types; licensed to develop, manufacture and commercialize LM-299, a novel investigational PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody from LaNova; and collaboration agreement with Eisai Co., Ltd., Bayer AG, and Ridgeback Biotherapeutics LP, as well Moderna, Inc. Merck & Co., Inc. has strategic collaboration with Infinimmune, Inc. to discover and develop antibodies against multiple therapeutic targets. The company was founded in 1891 and is headquartered in Rahway, New Jersey.
